anonymized_passage: before publishing a recommendation list, before future engagement and prediction error is known, an outside observer who does not control the decision selects a ranking rule subject to an exposure constraint using predicted relevance, the approved group indicator, and the exposure requirement. The stated objective is to maximize expected relevance subject to the stated exposure constraint, subject to the condition that the system cannot use attributes that are unavailable or prohibited at ranking time. The constraint reallocates exposure across eligible items and can displace higher predicted-relevance positions. Tighter exposure balance can improve representation while reducing predicted relevance under the model. The result concerns the specified exposure metric and does not establish broader social welfare. The study shows when the operational cost of the exposure requirement is concentrated in a few ranking positions.

model_or_study_facts: the system cannot use attributes that are unavailable or prohibited at ranking time
decisions: selects a ranking rule subject to an exposure constraint
context.objective: maximize expected relevance subject to the stated exposure constraint
decision_time_information: predicted relevance, the approved group indicator, and the exposure requirement
constraints: the system cannot use attributes that are unavailable or prohibited at ranking time
context.mechanism: The constraint reallocates exposure across eligible items and can displace higher predicted-relevance positions
supplied_evidence: Synthetic illustration; no external empirical evidence supplied.
